economics.reason: observed provider telemetry shows positive realized cache savings

aggregate.reason: observed provider telemetry shows positive realized cache savings

concentration.Recommendation: workload is flat (s<=1): vCache will not help — manufacture skew (canonicalize/aggregate) or abstain

recall.reason: cost gate refused: P·r >= S·U — recall-by-rebuild is net-negative (§11.0); send the unit cold

owner_slices.evidence: transcript/session telemetry has no fak compaction, KV-prefix reuse, or vDSO avoided-call counters

panels.name: base provider cache
panels.question: is the Anthropic prefix cache being reused at all?
panels.value: hit 66.0% — 80,000 cached input tokens served over 3 turns
panels.detail: the obvious base case — cache_read>0 every turn; this is what 'base cache works' means.

panels.name: M2 star anchors
panels.question: does a warmed prefix pay for itself within a family?
panels.value: saved 25.6% (31,000 token-equiv) across 1 families; first-positive turn 3
panels.detail: within-family reuse: the shared system prefix is warmed once and read by every later turn.

panels.name: M1 concentration
panels.question: is reuse CONCENTRATED enough for cross-family warming to help?
panels.detail: workload is flat (s<=1): vCache will not help — manufacture skew (canonicalize/aggregate) or abstain

panels.name: M1 warmth belief
panels.question: does the warmth estimator ever book a save it did not get?
panels.value: false-warm 0.00% (the lethal one) · false-cold 50.00% (benign under-claim) over 3 turns
panels.detail: false-warm is the dangerous direction (manifest says HIT, provider says MISS); 0% means verify-then-trust holds on real traffic.

panels.name: M3 dedicated warming
panels.question: should we spend a dedicated max_tokens:0 / decode-1 warm?
panels.value: 0/1 families net-positive from natural traffic by turn 1
panels.detail: the first natural request warms the prefix for free; a dedicated warm would only add a write — spend it only for latency or a TTL gap.

panels.name: M4 chains & recall
panels.question: should a single unit be recalled by replaying the chain?
panels.value: at the account's mean 40,430-token prefix: 404x loss, break-even 405 siblings
panels.detail: single-unit chain rebuild is a large net loss; the cost gate correctly refuses it and reserves rebuild for amortized fan-out.

panels.name: M5 governor
panels.question: pin, lazy-rebuild, or ride natural traffic per prefix?
panels.value: ride_natural (the dominant verdict across 1 families' observed arrival rates)
panels.detail: active sessions arrive faster than the 5m TTL (λT≥1), so natural traffic holds the prefix warm — no pin, no dedicated warm.

panels.name: score composite
panels.question: what grade does the run earn — measured vs synthetic?
panels.detail: same realized economics; the grade gap is almost entirely the concentration assumption (35 of 40 pts; the other 5 are the benign false-cold risk penalty) — synthetic steep (s=1.74) vs the account's measured-flat distribution.

panels.name: cachemeta canonicalization
panels.question: is the cacheable prefix staying byte-stable?
panels.value: inferred HOLDING — 0.00% false-warm (a canonicalization break would show as believed-warm, cache_read=0)
panels.detail: a volatile byte in the prefix (timestamp/UUID) would silently break reuse; 0 false-warms is the proxy that the prefix is canonical.
